question.text: Which of the following investments eligible under Section 80C has the SHORTEST lock-in period?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Among all Section 80C eligible investments, ELSS mutual funds have the shortest lock-in period of just 3 years. Tax-saving FDs and NSC have a 5-year lock-in, while PPF has a 15-year maturity (partial withdrawal allowed from year 7). SCSS has a 5-year tenure, and Sukanya Samriddhi Account matures when the girl child turns 21. **The Trap:** Candidates might confuse lock-in periods, especially between ELSS (3 years) and tax-saving FDs (5 years). Some may think NSC (5 years) is shorter than ELSS. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A MFD exam extensively tests ELSS features, including its 3-year lock-in as a key differentiator versus other 80C instruments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ELSS offers the unique combination of shortest lock-in (3 years), equity market exposure, and potential for higher returns among 80C options — making it the most recommended tax-saving instrument for growth-oriented investors.
